message:
refactor(llm): Standardize warning output through warn() helper

Problem:
- LLM module used raw console.warn() for retry messages, API key
  validation warnings, and fallback notifications
- This produced inconsistent formatting without the [cm] prefix
- Made tests brittle as they had to spy on console.warn specifically

Changes:
- Replace all console.warn() calls with warn() from utils.js
- warn() uses console.error() with consistent [cm] prefix formatting
- Update tests to spy on console.error instead of console.warn
- Fix test assertions to check the second argument (message content)
  since warn() calls console.error(prefix, message)

Also improved:
- generateObjectSafe() now detects network/rate-limit errors and
  re-throws them to let llmWithRetry handle backoff properly, rather
  than using its own retry logic for API errors
- This prevents double-retry and ensures consistent exponential backoff

The warn() helper ensures all CLI warnings have consistent formatting
and are sent to stderr (not stdout), making output parsing reliable.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
